title: soyeria pyrenaica
intro: Soyeria pyrenaica, also known as the "Spanish coffee", is a plant native to the Iberian Peninsula that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a stimulant and diuretic, and to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in compounds like gentiopicrin and gentiogenin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing pain and swelling, and protecting against oxidative stress. Soyeria pyrenaica can be prepared as a herbal infusion, tea, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine in the form of a decoction or infusion.
